source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the canonical Bridges to Prosperity (B2P) Bridge Builder Manual Vol. 1 (Suspended) architecture of a non-stiffened pedestrian footbridge: 2 main cables + 2 handrail cables running anchor-to-anchor over 2 saddle-topped towers; a timber deck composed of crossbeams and planks hung from suspender rods every 500 mm; 2 mass-gravity anchor blocks on each bank; cross-bracing and fencing wires between the handrails and main cables. No rulebook citations are added beyond those present in the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design one representative 80 m clear-span suspended (non-stiffened) pedestrian footbridge for a rural river crossing in a low-resource community, fully compliant with the Bridges to Prosperity (B2P) Bridge Builder Manual Vol. 1 (Suspended). The bridge must carry a uniformly distributed pedestrian live load of 4.07 kPa applied over the full 1.06 m clear walkway width, a lateral wind pressure of 1.44 kPa applied to the bridge envelope (deck + handrail mesh), and the self-weight of timber decking, suspender cables, steel handrail cables, and cross-bracing. The main carrying members are two main cables running from anchor block to anchor block over two masonry or reinforced concrete towers, and two handrail cables that also act as secondary load-bearing members. The main cable sag at mid-span under full dead + live load must fall between 5.0 percent and 6.0 percent of the clear span (i.e. 4.0 m to 4.8 m vertical sag for an 80 m span). Each main cable must be a 6x19 or 6x36 IWRC galvanized steel wire rope sized so that the maximum axial tension under factored dead + live + wind does not exceed one third of the cable minimum breaking load (cable safety factor >= 3.0 per B2P). The timber deck planks are 50 mm thick hardwood, spanning 1.06 m between crossbeams spaced 500 mm on center along the bridge length. Towers are 4.5-6.0 m tall masonry or reinforced concrete saddles anchored to a foundation block, and the back anchor blocks are mass-gravity concrete blocks sized from the anchor volume tables in the manual to resist the full horizontal + vertical cable pull with a sliding and overturning safety factor of at least 2.0. Deliverable is a single assembled bridge CAD model covering the full span (cables, towers, anchor blocks, deck, suspenders, handrails) with enough geometric detail to run a cable-catenary structural analysis and an anchor-block stability check. Vibration, thermal, fluid (CFD of wind), and radiation analyses are NOT required; the wind load is applied as an equivalent static pressure per manual. Mandatory cable-catenary analysis is a non-FEA solver (closed-form catenary + equilibrium) used in addition to the linear-elastic FEA on the deck, towers, anchors and suspenders.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The bridge is a classical B2P suspended (non-stiffened) footbridge delivered as a multi-part civil assembly: 2 main cables (left + right), 2 handrail cables (left + right), approximately 160 suspender rods at 500 mm pitch connecting main cables to the deck crossbeams, approximately 160 hardwood crossbeams carrying the deck planks, approximately 160 deck planks (50 mm hardwood spanning 1.06 m clear walkway), 2 mass-gravity concrete back-anchor blocks (one per bank), 2 masonry/RC tower saddles (one per bank), tower saddle plates carrying the main cables over the tower, and handrail-to-main-cable cross-bracing wires. Load paths: LC1 pedestrian live 4.07 kPa acts on planks -> crossbeams -> suspenders -> main cables -> tower saddles -> back-anchor blocks; LC2 lateral wind 1.44 kPa acts on the deck + handrail envelope -> crossbeams + handrail cables -> tower saddles (via side force) + anchor blocks (via combined H+V pull); LC3 self-weight is distributed through every member. The final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only (cable-catenary closed form + linear static FEA on deck/tower/anchor); per-part stress/tension is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part interface DCR at cable-to-suspender sockets, suspender-to-crossbeam U-bolts, cable-to-saddle friction, and anchor-block-to-cable pull must be <= 1.0. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, geometric windows (5-6% sag), and SF thresholds (3.0 cable; 2.0 anchor) from the original brief are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design one representative 80 m clear-span suspended (non-stiffened) pedestrian footbridge as a multi-part civil assembly (2 main cables, 2 handrail cables, ~160 suspender rods, ~160 hardwood crossbeams, ~160 hardwood deck planks, 2 mass-gravity concrete back-anchor blocks, 2 masonry/RC towers with saddle plates, cross-bracing) per the Bridges to Prosperity (B2P) Bridge Builder Manual Vol. 1 (Suspended).

Clear walkway 1.06 m, deck planks 50 mm hardwood, crossbeams and suspenders at 500 mm pitch, towers 4.8-6.0 m tall. Main cables are 6x19 or 6x36 IWRC galvanized steel wire rope.

Load cases: LC1 pedestrian live 4.07 kPa over the 1.06 m x 80 m walkway; LC2 lateral wind 1.44 kPa on bridge envelope; LC3 self-weight.

Pass/fail: main cable SF against MBL >= 3.0; midspan sag 5.0 percent to 6.0 percent of span (4.0 m to 4.8 m for 80 m); anchor-block sliding SF >= 2.0 and overturning SF >= 2.0. FEM at assembled level (cable-catenary closed form).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Main cable tension, sag, and anchor reactions on a non-stiffened suspended footbridge are solved by closed-form catenary + equilibrium per B2P manual, not by linear-elastic FEA.
examples: hand calc per Irvine cable theory

prompt.title: 80 m B2P suspended pedestrian footbridge (multi-part cable + deck + tower + anchor assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a B2P-compliant 80 m suspended footbridge as an assembled structure: 2 main cables, 2 handrail cables, ~160 suspenders, ~160 crossbeams, ~160 hardwood planks, 2 mass-gravity anchor blocks, 2 masonry/RC towers, saddle plates, and cross-bracing. Pedestrian 4.07 kPa + wind 1.44 kPa + dead load. Cable SF >= 3, anchor sliding + overturning SF >= 2, sag 5-6%. FEM at assembled level; interface DCR <= 1.0.

material.main_cables: 6x19 or 6x36 IWRC galvanized steel wire rope, nominal UTS 1770 MPa
material.suspender_rods: galvanized mild steel rod 10-16 mm dia, threaded ends
material.deck: tropical hardwood, design bending stress per B2P Table
material.crossbeam: tropical hardwood timber, 100 x 100 mm typical
material.saddle_plate: rolled steel plate with radiused groove for main cable

load_cases.name: pedestrian_live
load_cases.application: uniform over deck area (1.06 m x 80 m)

load_cases.name: wind_lateral
load_cases.application: lateral pressure on bridge envelope (deck edge + handrail mesh)

load_cases.name: dead
load_cases.magnitude: self-weight of cables, deck, towers, anchors, suspenders, handrails

boundary_conditions.main_cables: anchored at back-anchor blocks; pass over tower saddles as frictionless supports
boundary_conditions.anchor_blocks: founded on soil, resisting horizontal + vertical cable pull; reactions computed as dead + live + wind
boundary_conditions.deck: simply supported on transverse crossbeams hung from suspenders
boundary_conditions.handrail_cables: anchored at the same back-anchor blocks as main cables; also pass over tower saddles

objective.secondary: minimize concrete volume of anchor blocks and towers
objective.design_heuristic: Size main cables so that max tension under LC1 + LC2 + LC3 = (MBL/3); select sag at 5.5% to balance cable stress and pedestrian comfort.

output_format.body_count: multi-body (2 main cables + 2 handrail cables + ~160 suspenders + ~160 crossbeams + ~160 planks + 2 anchors + 2 towers + saddles)

parts.name: main_cable
parts.role: primary load-carrying wire rope running from one back-anchor block, over the near tower saddle, down the span as a catenary to a sag of 5-6% at midspan, up over the far tower saddle, and into the far back-anchor block; carries the full dead + live reaction in axial tension
parts.count_note: Left and right main cable (under the deck edge stringer lines).

geometric_constraints.termination: cable sockets (Crosby-style) at anchor block; seated in saddle groove over tower

parts.material_hint: galvanized steel wire rope 6x19 or 6x36 IWRC

parts.name: handrail_cable
parts.role: secondary load-bearing cable running parallel to and above the main cable at 1100 mm handrail height; carries lateral wind (LC2) and provides pedestrian handhold
parts.material_hint: galvanized steel wire rope (smaller than main)

parts.name: suspender_rod
parts.role: threaded mild-steel rod connecting main cable at the top to a crossbeam clip at the bottom, at 500 mm pitch along the span; transfers deck + pedestrian + wind reactions vertically (and through handrail ties horizontally) into the main cable
parts.count_note: Span 80 m / 500 mm pitch x 2 cables = 320 in theory; B2P typically uses single-rod pairs for crossbeams giving ~160 individually-indexed suspenders, with a pair of left/right attachments.

geometric_constraints.rod_length_mm_variable: varies with catenary position (shortest at midspan, longest near towers)

parts.material_hint: galvanized mild steel rod, rolled thread

parts.name: timber_crossbeam
parts.role: transverse hardwood timber spanning 1.06 m walkway clear width between the two suspender lines; carries the deck planks and transfers pedestrian/wind/dead load into suspenders
parts.count_note: One crossbeam per 500 mm pitch over 80 m = 160 crossbeams.

geometric_constraints.suspender_attachment: U-bolt through drilled hole, 2 per crossbeam
geometric_constraints.plank_attachment: through-bolted or lag-screwed to top face

parts.material_hint: tropical hardwood (e.g. ipê or eucalyptus), design bending stress per B2P Table

parts.name: deck_plank
parts.role: hardwood walking surface plank, 50 mm thick, spanning the 1.06 m clear walkway between the two crossbeam ends; carries the pedestrian live 4.07 kPa in bending
parts.count_note: One full-width plank per crossbeam at 500 mm pitch over 80 m, bolted or screwed to every crossbeam; teams may use longer planks spanning 2-3 crossbeams.

geometric_constraints.bending_stress_limit_MPa: per B2P Table for chosen hardwood

parts.name: masonry_or_RC_tower_with_saddle_plate
parts.role: 4.5-6.0 m tall tower (mortared masonry pier or RC column) at each bank carrying a steel saddle plate over which the main cables pass; redirects the catenary cable force and passes vertical reaction into the tower foundation
parts.count_note: One tower per bank (2 total); each tower carries 2 main cable saddles (left + right).

geometric_constraints.saddle_plate_material: rolled steel plate, groove radius >= 10x cable diameter

parts.material_hint: mortared masonry or reinforced concrete; rolled steel saddle plate

parts.name: mass_gravity_anchor_block
parts.role: back-anchor concrete gravity block on each bank that resists the full horizontal + vertical pull of the main + handrail cables; sized from B2P anchor volume tables to achieve sliding SF >= 2 and overturning SF >= 2
parts.count_note: One anchor block per bank; each anchor receives 2 main cables + 2 handrail cables plus any fencing ties.
parts.material_hint: mass concrete (no primary reinforcing required beyond cable socket embedment)

parts.name: cross_bracing_and_fencing_wire
parts.role: diagonal wire or rod ties between the main cable and the handrail cable at regular intervals (typically every 5 m); stiffen the bridge laterally, prevent pedestrian fall, and improve wind stability
parts.count_note: At 5 m pitch over 80 m = 16 cross-braces per side; plus continuous fencing mesh.
parts.material_hint: galvanized wire / rod + chain-link fencing mesh

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: B2P Bridge Builder Manual minimum cable SF vs MBL
B2P suspended-bridge geometric window for pedestrian comfort and cable stress

U-bolt / cable-clip / saddle-friction / anchor-socket DCR on every suspender-to-cable, suspender-to-crossbeam, cable-to-saddle, and cable-to-anchor-socket connection must remain <= 1.0 under factored LC1 + LC2 + LC3.
Handrail cables must be tied to main cables at <= 5 m pitch via H-family cross-bracing to prevent lateral sway amplification under LC2 wind.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: cable elements (main + handrail + suspenders), beam elements (crossbeams, cross-bracing rods), shell (deck planks), solid (anchor block, tower, saddle plate)
analysis_types: rigid body stability calculation for anchor block (non-FEA check)
recommended_fea_setup.contact_modeling: friction contact at cable-to-saddle groove; U-bolt preload at cable-to-suspender; lock-nut preload at suspender-to-crossbeam

interfaces.mediated_by: wire-rope U-bolt or Crosby clip clamping suspender rod C to main cable A
interfaces.load_path: Vertical reaction from crossbeam/deck into main cable via the suspender; U-bolt preload must be sized per B2P against slip under combined LC1 + LC3

interfaces.mediated_by: lock-nut pair on threaded suspender end through a drilled crossbeam hole, bearing on washers
interfaces.load_path: Deck + pedestrian live load from crossbeam D transferred up through suspender C as axial tension

interfaces.mediated_by: through-bolt or lag-screw clamping hardwood plank E to the top face of crossbeam D
interfaces.load_path: LC1 pedestrian live load enters plank in bending, reacts into crossbeam as shear + bearing

interfaces.mediated_by: main cable seated in a radiused steel saddle plate on top of tower F; friction + saddle geometry redirect cable force
interfaces.load_path: Vertical component of cable tension passes into tower as compression; horizontal component balanced between the back-span and river-span of the cable

interfaces.mediated_by: cable socket (open spelter or swaged) embedded in anchor block G concrete
interfaces.load_path: Full resultant of main cable force into anchor block; block geometry provides sliding and overturning resistance per B2P tables

interfaces.mediated_by: cross-bracing wire / rod H tied to handrail cable B and to main cable A at 5 m pitch
interfaces.load_path: LC2 lateral wind load on handrail / fencing distributed into main cable via H; reduces lateral cable sway

fem_check_policy.rationale: A B2P suspended footbridge is a canonical whole-system check: cable tension depends on deck mass + live load + handrail tie stiffness, anchor-block sliding SF depends on combined H+V pull from both main and handrail cables, and sag (R2) is inherently a geometric property of the assembled bridge. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The B2P Bridge Builder Manual explicitly decomposes the bridge into main cables, handrail cables, suspenders, crossbeams, deck planks, towers with saddles, and mass-gravity anchor blocks, each sized from a separate table. The sag window (5-6%) and the anchor SFs (>=2) are only defined on the assembled structure.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact counts of suspenders, crossbeams, and planks (approx 160 each for 80 m at 500 mm pitch) are speculative; cross-bracing pitch (5 m) is a B2P manual default. Anchor-block concrete volume depends on local soil properties.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: B2P suspended footbridges are sized by static live + wind; pedestrian-induced vibration is mitigated by geometric sag/span limits, not by modal FEA in the manual.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Cable thermal expansion is covered by construction pre-tension rules, not a thermal FEA.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Wind is applied as equivalent static 1.44 kPa per manual; no CFD required.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Scour and flood freeboard above 100-year flood elevation
Pedestrian-induced lateral and vertical vibration (Eurocode 5 Annex B)
Corrosion allowance for decades of field exposure

eval_coverage: cable-catenary non-FEA solver coupled with structural FEA
explicit load values (4.07 kPa / 1.44 kPa) baked in
anchor-block stability with sliding + overturning SF
humanitarian civil scope (low-resource materials, masonry + steel rope + hardwood)
multi-part civil assembly with cables + deck + towers + anchors
interface DCR at U-bolts, saddle friction, and anchor socket
